subtopics.name: Particles in the atom and atomic radius
learning_outcomes: Understand that atoms are mostly empty space surrounding a very small, dense nucleus containing protons and neutrons.
Electrons are found in shells in the empty space around the nucleus.
Identify and describe protons, neutrons, and electrons in terms of relative charges and relative masses.
Understand the terms atomic and proton number; mass and nucleon number.
Describe the distribution of mass and charge within an atom.
Describe the behaviour of beams of protons, neutrons, and electrons moving at the same velocity in an electric field.
Determine the numbers of protons, neutrons, and electrons in atoms and ions given atomic number, mass number, and charge.
State and explain qualitatively the variations in atomic and ionic radius across a period and down a group.

subtopics.name: Isotopes
learning_outcomes: Define the term isotope in terms of numbers of protons and neutrons.
Understand the notation ^A_Z X for isotopes, where A is nucleon number and Z is proton number.
State and explain why isotopes of the same element have the same chemical properties.
State and explain why isotopes have different physical properties (limited to mass and density).

subtopics.name: Electrons, energy levels and atomic orbitals
learning_outcomes: Understand the terms: shells, sub-shells, orbitals, principal quantum number (n), and ground state.
Describe the number of orbitals in s, p, and d sub-shells and the number of electrons that fill them.
Describe the order of increasing energy of sub-shells within the first three shells and the 4s and 4p sub-shells.
Describe electronic configurations including the number of electrons in each shell, sub-shell, and orbital.
Explain electronic configurations in terms of electron energy and inter-electron repulsion.
Determine the electronic configuration of atoms and ions from hydrogen to krypton.
Understand and use the 'electrons in boxes' notation.
Describe and sketch the shapes of s and p orbitals.
Describe a free radical as a species with one or more unpaired electrons.

subtopics.name: Ionisation energy
learning_outcomes: Define and use the term first ionisation energy (IE).
Construct equations for first, second, and subsequent ionisation energies.
Identify and explain trends in ionisation energies across a period and down a group.
Identify and explain the variation in successive ionisation energies of an element.
Understand that ionisation energies are due to attraction between the nucleus and the outer electron.
Explain factors influencing ionisation energy: nuclear charge, radius, shielding, and spin-pair repulsion.
Deduce electronic configurations and Periodic Table positions using successive ionisation energy data.

subtopics.name: Relative masses of atoms and molecules
learning_outcomes: Define the unified atomic mass unit as one-twelfth of the mass of a carbon-12 atom.
Define relative atomic mass (Ar), relative isotopic mass, relative molecular mass (Mr), and relative formula mass.

subtopics.name: The mole and the Avogadro constant
learning_outcomes: Define and use the term mole in terms of the Avogadro constant.

subtopics.name: Formulas
learning_outcomes: Write formulas of ionic compounds from charges and oxidation numbers.
Predict ionic charge from Periodic Table position.
Recall names and formulas for specific ions: NO3-, CO32-, SO42-, OH-, NH4+, Zn2+, Ag+, HCO3-, PO43-.
Write balanced chemical and ionic equations (excluding spectator ions) with state symbols.
Define and use the terms empirical formula, molecular formula, anhydrous, hydrated, and water of crystallisation.
Calculate empirical and molecular formulas from given data.

subtopics.name: Reacting masses and volumes
learning_outcomes: Perform calculations involving reacting masses, percentage yield, gas volumes, solution concentrations, and limiting/excess reagents.
Deduce stoichiometric relationships from these calculations.

subtopics.name: Electronegativity and bonding
learning_outcomes: Define electronegativity as the power of an atom to attract electrons to itself.
Explain factors influencing electronegativity: nuclear charge, radius, and shielding.
State and explain trends in electronegativity across a period and down a group.
Use Pauling electronegativity differences to predict ionic or covalent bond formation.

subtopics.name: Ionic bonding
learning_outcomes: Define ionic bonding as the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions.
Describe ionic bonding in NaCl, MgO, and CaF2.

subtopics.name: Metallic bonding
learning_outcomes: Define metallic bonding as the electrostatic attraction between positive metal ions and delocalised electrons.

subtopics.name: Covalent and coordinate bonding
learning_outcomes: Define covalent bonding as electrostatic attraction between the nuclei of two atoms and a shared pair of electrons.
Describe covalent bonding in H2, O2, N2, Cl2, HCl, CO2, NH3, CH4, C2H6, and C2H4.
Understand octet expansion in Period 3 elements like SO2, PCl5, and SF6.
Describe coordinate (dative) bonding in NH4+ and Al2Cl6.
Describe σ (direct overlap) and π (sideways overlap of p orbitals) bonds.
Use the concept of hybridisation to describe sp, sp2, and sp3 orbitals.
Define and use bond energy and bond length to compare reactivity.

subtopics.name: Shapes of molecules
learning_outcomes: Use VSEPR theory to explain shapes and bond angles (e.g., BF3, CO2, CH4, NH3, H2O, SF6, PF5).

subtopics.name: Intermolecular forces
learning_outcomes: Describe hydrogen bonding (limited to N-H and O-H groups) and explain the anomalous properties of H2O.
Use electronegativity to explain bond polarity and dipole moments.
Describe van der Waals' forces, including London dispersion forces and permanent dipole-permanent dipole forces.
Understand that intramolecular bonds (ionic, covalent, metallic) are generally stronger than intermolecular forces.

subtopics.name: Dot-and-cross diagrams
learning_outcomes: Use dot-and-cross diagrams to illustrate ionic, covalent, and coordinate bonding.

subtopics.name: The gaseous state
learning_outcomes: Explain gas pressure in terms of molecular collisions with container walls.
Understand ideal gas assumptions: zero particle volume and no intermolecular forces.
State and use the ideal gas equation pV=nRT.

subtopics.name: Bonding and structure
learning_outcomes: Describe lattice structures: giant ionic (NaCl, MgO), simple molecular (I2, C60, ice), giant molecular (SiO2, graphite, diamond), and giant metallic (Cu).
Predict the effect of structure and bonding on physical properties (melting/boiling point, conductivity, solubility).

subtopics.name: Enthalpy change, ΔH
learning_outcomes: Understand exothermic (ΔH negative) and endothermic (ΔH positive) reactions.
Construct and interpret reaction pathway diagrams.
Define standard conditions (298K, 101 kPa) and enthalpy changes of reaction, formation, combustion, and neutralisation.
Calculate enthalpy changes using bond energies and experimental results (q=mcΔT).

subtopics.name: Hess’s law
learning_outcomes: Apply Hess’s law to construct energy cycles and perform calculations.

subtopics.name: Redox processes
learning_outcomes: Calculate oxidation numbers and use them to balance equations.
Define and use terms: redox, oxidation, reduction, disproportionation, oxidising agent, and reducing agent.

subtopics.name: Chemical equilibria
learning_outcomes: Understand reversible reactions and dynamic equilibrium in closed systems.
Use Le Chatelier's principle to deduce effects of changes in temperature, concentration, pressure, or catalysts.
Deduce expressions for and perform calculations with Kc and Kp.
Explain conditions used in the Haber and Contact processes.

subtopics.name: Brønsted–Lowry theory of acids and bases
learning_outcomes: Recall names/formulas of common acids and alkalis.
Describe Brønsted–Lowry theory and distinguish between strong and weak acids/bases.
Understand neutralisation and salt formation.
Sketch pH titration curves and select suitable indicators.

subtopics.name: Rate of reaction
learning_outcomes: Explain rate in terms of collision frequency and effective collisions.
Explain effects of concentration and pressure on reaction rates.

subtopics.name: Effect of temperature
learning_outcomes: Define activation energy (EA).
Use Boltzmann distributions to explain the effect of temperature on rates.

subtopics.name: Catalysts
learning_outcomes: Explain how catalysts provide an alternative mechanism with lower activation energy.
Interpret reaction pathway diagrams for catalyzed reactions.

subtopics.name: Periodicity of physical properties in Period 3
learning_outcomes: Describe variations in atomic radius, ionic radius, melting point, and electrical conductivity.
Explain variations in melting point and conductivity based on structure and bonding.

subtopics.name: Periodicity of chemical properties in Period 3
learning_outcomes: Describe reactions of Period 3 elements with oxygen, chlorine, and water.
Explain variation in oxidation numbers and acid/base behavior of oxides and hydroxides.
Describe reactions of chlorides and oxides with water, including pH of resulting solutions.

subtopics.name: Chemical periodicity of other elements
learning_outcomes: Predict properties of elements in a group and identify unknown elements based on trends.

subtopics.name: Group 2 Metals and Compounds
learning_outcomes: Describe reactions of Group 2 metals (Mg to Ba) with oxygen, water, and acids.
Describe reactions of Group 2 oxides, hydroxides, and carbonates.
Describe the trend in thermal stability of nitrates and carbonates.
State trends in solubility for hydroxides and sulfates.

subtopics.name: Halogens and Halides
learning_outcomes: Describe trends in colour, volatility, and bond strength of halogens.
Describe relative reactivity as oxidising agents and in reactions with hydrogen.
Explain thermal stability of hydrogen halides in terms of bond strength.
Describe reactivity of halide ions as reducing agents and their reactions with silver ions/ammonia and sulfuric acid.
Describe disproportionation of chlorine in cold/hot NaOH and its use in water purification.

subtopics.name: Compounds of Nitrogen and Sulfur
learning_outcomes: Explain the lack of reactivity of N2.
Describe the basicity of ammonia and the structure/formation of the ammonium ion.
Explain occurrences of nitrogen oxides and their role in catalytic converters, photochemical smog, and acid rain.

topic_name: Introduction to AS Level organic chemistry

subtopics.name: Formulas, functional groups and naming
learning_outcomes: Define hydrocarbon and understand functional groups.
Use general, structural, displayed, and skeletal formulas.
Use systematic nomenclature for molecules up to six carbon atoms.

subtopics.name: Characteristic organic reactions
learning_outcomes: Use terminology: homologous series, saturated/unsaturated, fission (homolytic/heterolytic), and free radicals.
Understand reaction types: addition, substitution, elimination, hydrolysis, condensation, oxidation, and reduction.
Understand mechanisms: free-radical substitution, electrophilic addition, nucleophilic substitution, and nucleophilic addition.

subtopics.name: Shapes and bonding
learning_outcomes: Describe straight-chain, branched, and cyclic molecules.
Explain shapes and bond angles using sp, sp2, and sp3 hybridisation.

subtopics.name: Isomerism
learning_outcomes: Describe structural isomerism (chain, positional, functional group).
Describe stereoisomerism: geometrical (cis/trans) and optical (chiral centres/enantiomers).

subtopics.name: Alkanes
learning_outcomes: Recall production (hydrogenation, cracking), combustion, and free-radical substitution mechanisms.

subtopics.name: Alkenes
learning_outcomes: Recall production (elimination, dehydration), electrophilic addition reactions, and oxidation with KMnO4. Explain Markovnikov addition.

subtopics.name: Halogenoalkanes
learning_outcomes: Classify as primary/secondary/tertiary; describe nucleophilic substitution (SN1 and SN2) and elimination.

subtopics.name: Alcohols
learning_outcomes: Recall production and reactions (combustion, substitution, oxidation, dehydration, esterification). Use the tri-iodomethane test.

subtopics.name: Carbonyls
learning_outcomes: Produce aldehydes/ketones by oxidation; describe reduction and nucleophilic addition with HCN. Use 2,4-DNPH, Fehling’s, and Tollens’ reagents.

subtopics.name: Carboxylic acids/Esters
learning_outcomes: Recall production, redox, neutralisation, and esterification.

subtopics.name: Nitrogen compounds
learning_outcomes: Produce primary amines and nitriles.

subtopics.name: Polymerisation
learning_outcomes: Describe addition polymerisation (e.g., poly(ethene), PVC) and disposal issues.

subtopics.name: Synthetic Routes
learning_outcomes: Identify functional groups and devise multi-step synthetic routes.

subtopics.name: Infrared spectroscopy
learning_outcomes: Identify functional groups from a spectrum.

subtopics.name: Mass spectrometry
learning_outcomes: Calculate Ar, deduce molecular mass from the molecular ion peak, and identify fragments. Use [M+1]+ to find carbon atoms and [M+2]+ for Br/Cl.
